trade.summary: China is the Netherlands' third-largest trading partner. Netherlands is a major European entry point for Chinese goods via Rotterdam. Netherlands exports high-tech equipment (ASML chip machines), food products, and chemicals. China exports electronics, machinery, and consumer goods. The semiconductor equipment trade is of unique strategic significance.
disputes: Dutch export controls on ASML EUV/DUV machines to China (aligned with US)
Chinese opposition to Dutch semiconductor export restrictions
Dutch concerns about Chinese espionage (especially at ASML)

diplomatic.summary: Relations have been severely strained by the Netherlands' decision to restrict exports of ASML semiconductor equipment to China, aligned with US export controls. China has repeatedly pressured the Netherlands to reverse this policy. The Netherlands is a vocal critic of China on human rights (Xinjiang, Hong Kong) and has expelled Chinese intelligence officers. Dutch public opinion has become more negative toward China.
recent_incidents: Netherlands restricted ASML EUV exports to China (2023)
Netherlands expanded export controls to older DUV ASML machines (2024)
China retaliated with restrictions on gallium and germanium exports critical for chip manufacturing
Dutch intelligence expelled Chinese spies targeting ASML
Netherlands aligned with EU EV tariffs

military.summary: No military alliance. Netherlands is a NATO member and China is designated a systemic challenge by NATO. Netherlands participates in South China Sea freedom of navigation operations. No arms transfers (EU embargo). Dutch frigate operated alongside US carrier near Taiwan (2024).
incidents: Dutch frigate participated in Taiwan Strait transit (2024)
Netherlands joined US-led semiconductor export control coordination

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Chinese companies use Dutch holding structures for European operations. Dutch pension funds are reducing China exposure. Chinese investment in Dutch tech companies is under increasing scrutiny. Rotterdam remains a major hub for Chinese trade with Europe.

energy.summary: Netherlands imports Chinese solar panels and batteries. Dutch Shell and other energy firms have China operations. Some LNG trade via Rotterdam. China imports Dutch food and agricultural products.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation under growing scrutiny. Dutch universities are reviewing Chinese partnerships for dual-use risks. ASML technology espionage cases have heightened concern. Joint publications declining as Netherlands tightens research security.
